idea.hook: Jupiter's most famous storm... is SHRINKING?! 🤯
facts: In the 1800s, Jupiter's Great Red Spot was estimated to be over 40,000 km wide.
At that colossal size, you could have fit *three* Earths (each ~12,742 km wide) inside it!
Today, this iconic storm has dwindled to only about 15,000 km across.
idea.payoff: This epic, centuries-old monster storm is slowly fading away before our eyes!
idea.title: Jupiter's GIANT Spot is DISAPPEARING! 😱🪐

scenes.text: Jupiter's most famous storm... is SHRINKING?! 🤯
scenes.visual: A vibrant, full-screen image of Jupiter, with its iconic Great Red Spot prominently featured. Stars subtly twinkling in the background.

scenes.text: 1800s: Great Red Spot was >40,000 km wide!
scenes.visual: A close-up view of Jupiter's Great Red Spot. An animated measurement bar appears across the spot, showing '40,000 km'.

scenes.text: You could fit THREE Earths inside it! 🌎🌎🌎
scenes.visual: The Great Red Spot remains on screen. Three animated Earths (or Earth silhouettes) slide into the spot, fitting comfortably side-by-side to demonstrate scale.
The Great Red Spot visibly shrinks on screen. The three Earths disappear. A new, smaller animated measurement bar appears across the now smaller spot, showing '~15,000 km'.

scenes.text: This epic storm is slowly FADING AWAY before our eyes!
scenes.visual: A wider shot of Jupiter. The Great Red Spot looks distinctly smaller and perhaps slightly less vivid. A subtle, slow fade effect on the Great Red Spot itself to emphasize 'fading away'.
